Gym owners, what's going on? So after you close the sale, so this is immediately post sale, there are three things that need to happen. First, mind out, I've actually closed the sale, the card may not work, there's a million things that can go wrong, so try and process the sale at the point of sale, okay? That may seem like a no-brainer, but honestly it happens to people, Aldenburg puts down a payment and they don't actually process it and they end up losing the sale, alright? So don't let that happen, process the payment. Alright, the second thing is that you want them to get to the appointment, alright? And so just some fun rules of thought here is that when you schedule their win, whenever someone's up that has to happen, always schedule it then. So when they come in for their win, you're going to schedule their next workout. When they go to their, the win to happen is going to happen, and so that way you don't have to have some coordination that happens in between, it saves everyone tons of time in headache, alright? So the next thing at the end of the sale is you're going to say, awesome, I'm going to see you at set. And so typically we do these on Saturday mornings early morning, so before your sales apply, it's the best time to do this, and you can start again after one if you have enough sales that you have to do more. And then Monday evening, so usually most people can make the Saturday morning exception do it late Monday. How many people per time slot should you use? I recommend doing max two people per 30 minute time slot. That means that you can do four people per hour, so people, if you're doing three hours, you can do 12, etc. And that usually can handle most people's sales volume. Well, now you're at 16 a week. If you're doing 16 a week, then you're good. That being said, if you do more than that, which some of our guys do, you can always just set more times. 30 minute time slot seems to work very well in being efficient during the way in process. And the last piece here is you want to reinforce the sale. And so especially if you're not the person who closed, you want to text them a personal welcome message. So if you have their number, which you should because they signed a contract, text them just saying like, hey, I'm the owner of SuperRaview. I see you're starting here on Saturday at 9 a.m. So you just want to just give them a little hug. It's going above and beyond, and they will appreciate it more than I can tell you. So just a quick recap. As soon as you finish the sale, process the damn payment. Excuse me. Second, make sure you get them scheduled for the new year. Make sure you wow them so they don't get cold feet and want to back out. So when you close the sale, do those three things, and you should be good to go.
